data.title: Trinseo PLC 1Q 2026: Revenue $724.7M, EPS ($3.2) — 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Trinseo PLC reported first-quarter 2026 results with net sales of $724.7M, a year-over-year decline, and a net loss of ($115.9M) that produced diluted loss per share of ($3.2) for the quarter.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$724.7M$784.8M(7.7%)
Net income²($115.9M)($79M)(46.7%)
Diluted EPS³($3.2)($2.22)(44.1%)

¹ Reported as “Net sales”. ² Reported as “Net loss”. ³ Reported as “loss per share‒diluted”.

Business Highlights

  • Demand & sales trend: Net sales fell about 8% YoY due to weaker end‑market demand, lower pricing and reduced volumes across segments.
  • Segment performance: Engineered Materials delivered margin improvement and a roughly 31% increase in adjusted EBITDA, while Polymer Solutions and Latex Binders experienced margin pressure.
  • Cost and operations: Restructuring actions taken in 2025 lowered fixed costs and helped support EBITDA amid difficult market conditions.
  • Market dynamics: Geopolitical volatility and feedstock disruptions limited near‑term recovery but created selective opportunities for volume capture.
  • Liquidity and working capital: Working capital stress and contraction in trade credit materially affected operations and cash conversion in Q1.

Original SEC Filing: Trinseo PLC [ TSEOF ] - 10-Q - Apr. 30, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Southern Copper Corp 1Q 2026: Revenue $4.25B, EPS $1.92— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Southern Copper Corp reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue rising to $4.25B and diluted EPS of $1.92, driven by higher metal prices and increased by‑product sales that lifted profitability versus the year-ago quarter.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$4.25B$3.12B36.2%
Net income²$1.58B$945.9M66.7%
Diluted EPS³$1.92$1.1567%

¹ Reported as “Net sales (including sales to related parties, see note 5)”. ² Reported as “Net income attributable to SCC”. ³ Reported as “Per common share amounts attributable to SCC”.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth of 36.2% was driven by higher metal prices and stronger by‑product sales; copper's share of sales fell to 70.2% as silver (12.5%) and molybdenum (10.5%) gained.
  • Production: copper output declined 4.0% to 508.3M lbs, while silver rose 11.1% and zinc increased 2.0% year over year.
  • Capital spending totaled $441.9M in the quarter; the Tía María project is 32.5% complete with 4,207 construction jobs, and the company continues advancing multiple Peruvian and Mexican projects.
  • Company maintained ESG momentum with recognition in the S&P Sustainability Yearbook and from Sustainalytics, and received community and water conservation awards.

Original SEC Filing: SOUTHERN COPPER CORP/ [ SCCO ] - 10-Q - Apr. 30, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: CDC Warns Of Drug-Resistant Salmonella Linked To Backyard Birds
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Dozens of people have been sickened and 13 hospitalized by the same strain of Salmonella infection linked to backyard poultry, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

The CDC first reported the outbreak late last week. So far, more than 30 cases have been identified across multiple states, with several hospitalizations. In some instances, the bacteria have also shown resistance to multiple antibiotics.

As of April 23, a total of 34 illnesses had been reported in 13 states. Among the 27 cases with detailed information, no deaths have been reported. Health officials note that the true number of infections is likely higher, as many people with mild symptoms do not seek medical care.

Laboratory testing shows that all patient samples are resistant to fosfomycin, a drug often used when other treatments fail. In eight cases, the bacteria also showed resistance to additional antibiotics, including chloramphenicol, streptomycin, sulfisoxazole, and tetracycline.

The outbreak has been linked to backyard poultry such as chickens and ducks. Even when the birds appear healthy and clean, they can still carry salmonella. People may become infected by handling the animals or anything they come into contact with, then touching their mouth or eating without washing their hands.

The CDC is urging backyard poultry owners to take precautionary measures, such as washing hands thoroughly with soap and water after handling birds, eggs, or related equipment; keeping poultry outdoors; and using dedicated footwear when caring for them. The agency also advises against close contact, such as kissing or snuggling birds near the face.

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Is Qualcomm Ready for Primetime? What the Technicals Say. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Doug Busch

In a market increasingly defined by connectivity, compute, and edge intelligence, Qualcomm is reasserting itself as a central enabler of the next wave of growth.

Long viewed primarily through the lens of smartphones, the company is now demonstrating broader earnings power across automotive, the internet of things, and AI-driven applications. With multiple end markets stabilizing and new revenue streams gaining traction, the narrative is shifting from cyclical recovery to structural expansion, positioning Qualcomm as a more durable and diversified growth story than the market has historically priced in.

To be frank, Qualcomm has lagged behind an otherwise strong semiconductor group, but the current setup suggests that it may join the broader rally. Of course, the iShares Semiconductor exchange-traded fund recently recorded an 18-session winning streak, its longest run since the fund's inception in 2021. Qualcomm came into Thursday's session down 8% year to date, but will now have some wind at its back. Peers Nvidia and NXPI Semiconductors have made nice moves lately: Nvidia broke above the $200 level in a double-bottom base, and NXPI jumped more than 25% — its best day since 2010 — following a jubilant earnings release.

The stock's daily chart shows a bullish RSI divergence that formed with a higher RSI low as price made a lower low in February and April. That suggested downside momentum was waning even as the stock trended lower.

True to form, the stock rebounded nicely and is now seeking its first four-week winning streak in almost two years. A bullish island reversal was completed with the 11% gap up on April 24 (following an 8.5% gap down on Feb. 5 after its seventh consecutive negative earnings reaction). In early trading, Qualcomm is up 12% after a well-received report, attempting to put that streak behind it. It is approaching a double-bottom pivot of $184.55 as it pushes above the 200-day simple moving average resistance this week. The 50-day simple moving average has also begun to slope higher for the first time since late last year. The stock could reach $265 by early 2027, representing a 43% gain from current prices. Remain bullish above $156.

Qualcomm was trading around $183 Thursday.

Looking at the weekly stock chart over the past five years, one can see how round-number theory came into play over an 18-month period. A bearish descending triangle formed between the summer of 2022 and late 2023, featuring a triple bottom just above the $100 level. The stock broke above the pattern. This is a bullish sign, as false moves are often followed quickly by moves in the opposite direction. It then more than doubled over the next eight months, until a bearish shooting star candle formed in June 2024. That started a current weekly double-bottom base with a bullish piercing line candle the week ended April 11, 2025, and the lows earlier in April. Notice the strong volume over the past two weeks, with a 9% gain last week and an additional 5% gain this week so far.

Momentum is building as Qualcomm breaks out, suggesting the path of least resistance is higher.

Doug Busch is the senior technical analyst at Barron's Investor Circle . His technical view is added to stock picks, including those published exclusively for Investor Circle readers. A glossary of technical terms is updated regularly with new entries.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Trump-Backed WLFI Token Hits Record Low Amid Controversial Governance Vote To Lock Up 63 Billion Tokens
data.description:
Key points:
  • WLFI fell more than 7% to a record low of $0.059 after a governance proposal to lock up over 63 billion tokens entered the voting phase.
  • Early voting shows strong support, with 99.95% of votes in favor and quorum already reached.
  • Retail sentiment on Stocktwits turned ‘bullish’ despite the price decline, and chatter rose to ‘high’ from ‘extremely low’ levels. 

Trump family-backed World Liberty Financial's WLFI token plummeted to a record low on Thursday after the cryptocurrency project’s new governance vote to lock up more than 63 billion WLFI tokens went live.

WLFI’s price fell over 7% in the last 24 hours to around $0.059 in the last 24 hours, marking an all-time low for the cryptocurrency token, according to CoinGecko data. On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around the coin however flipped to ‘bullish’ from ‘bearish over the past day. Chatter rose to ‘high’ from ‘extremely low’ levels.

Meanwhile, Bitcoin (BTC) rose 1.4% in the last 24 hours to around $76,200. Retail sentiment around the apex cryptocurrency trended in 'neutral' territory over the past day, accompanied by chatter at 'normal' levels.

Why Was The WLFI Token Falling?

The dip came after WLFI’s governance proposal that would lock up more than 63 billion WLFI tokens opened for community voting on Wednesday afternoon. Under the plan, early investors face a two-year lockup cliff, followed by two more years of gradual release. Founders, team members, and advisers would be subject to the same initial two-year cliff, with a longer three-year linear vesting schedule afterward.https://x.com/worldlibertyfi/status/2049541997132701777

Retail Traders Back Proposal While Critics Raise Concerns

Many retail traders on Stocktwits supported the proposal and anticipated the token could cross $1 in the next few months.

https://www.stocktwits.com/OldSheldon/message/651890069

https://www.stocktwits.com/bubbles1811/message/651903329

On-chain data showed strong early support. Roughly 99.93% of votes cast so far are in favor of the proposal. The required quorum of 1 billion tokens has already been met, with about over 7 billion tokens backing the measure and just 5.2 million opposed.

In post on X, Simon Dedic, founder of Moonrock Capital, described the proposal as resembling a rug pull and questioned its timing. He noted that the two-year lockup period broadly aligns with the remainder of Donald Trump’s current term in office.

Tron founder Justin Sun, who recently filed a lawsuit against WLFI, called it one of the “most absurd” proposals he had ever come across.

President Donald Trump is listed that the co-founder emeritus on the project’s official website. Donald Trump Jr. and Eric Trump are also listed as co-founders. Trump’s youngest son, Barron Trump, is the ‘DeFi Visionary’ and Web3 Ambassador of the project.

data.title: Camp Mystic Won't Reopen This Summer After Withdrawing License Application — WSJ
data.description:
By Joseph Pisani

The Texas camp where 27 girls died in catastrophic flooding last year won't be reopening this summer as planned.

Camp Mystic said Thursday that it withdrew its application for a license to open this summer after Texas authorities told the all-girls camp last week that its emergency plans fell short in more than 20 categories.

"No administrative process or summer season should move forward while families continue to grieve, while investigations continue and while so many Texans still carry the pain of last July's tragedy," Camp Mystic said in a statement.

The camp had planned to reopen for its 100th season on May 30 at a separate site that wasn't affected by the floods and where no children died. Some 850 campers were expected.

Floods killed 27 girls at the Texas camp last July after torrential rains caused the nearby Guadalupe River to rise.

Updates to follow as news develops.

Write to Joseph Pisani at joseph.pisani@wsj.com

data.title: US FDA approves Axsome's drug for Alzheimer's-related agitation
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has approved Axsome Therapeutics' drug to treat agitation in patients with Alzheimer's disease, the company said on Friday.

The drug, branded as Auvelity, offers a new treatment option for patients with Alzheimer's-related agitation, marked by restlessness, aggression and emotional distress. These patients have long relied on antipsychotic drugs, which had serious safety risks.

Alzheimer's disease, the most common form of dementia, affects about 7 million people in the United States. Agitation occurs in up to 70% of the patients, contributing to faster cognitive decline, heavier burden on caregivers, and higher risk of death.

data.title: Payment networks spotlight spending resilience with upbeat quarter
data.description:
By Utkarsh Shetti

Mastercard joined Visa and American Express in posting a quarterly profit beat on Thursday, highlighting spending resilience as well as allaying fears of a slowdown in the face of growing pressures.

Payment processors offer the earliest window into consumer spending health, given their mammoth market share in facilitating transactions on their individual networks.

Spending has largely held up despite concerns of economic uncertainty fueled by the Iran war and U.S. tariffs, even though consumer confidence has waned in a sluggish labor market.

American Express , whose customer base is typically seen as more affluent, topped first-quarter profit expectations last week, while Visa posted a quarterly profit beat on Tuesday.

"If you were hoping for a recessionary tell, the payment processors did not get the memo," said Michael Ashley Schulman, Partner at Cerity Partners.

Executives at all three firms sought to inspire confidence in the health of the consumer, underscoring that spending had remained resilient despite economic pressures.

Concerns are, however, growing that the resilience may be challenged by a rapid rise in global energy prices that is clouding the economic outlook and complicating policy decisions.

SPENDING STILL RESILIENT

A bulk of consumer spending comes from wealthier households, which continue to make discretionary purchases, while lower-income families pare back on non-essential items.

Experts are increasingly highlighting the bifurcation as the so-called "K-shaped" economy continues to underpin consumer trends, cushioning industries such as travel and entertainment.

The war in Iran initially battered stock markets, but those losses have largely been recouped in hopes that the conflict will be short-lived. Market declines typically temper spending by wealthy consumers.

"With the market rebound, higher-income individuals have been back to business as usual with spending," said Brian Jacobsen, chief economic strategist at Annex Wealth Management.

Earlier this month, most of the big U.S. lenders also reported an uptick in consumer loan balances, signaling sustained borrowing despite macroeconomic pressures that typically prompt caution.

Spending growth aside, underlying credit quality has also not deteriorated, Evercore ISI analyst Adam Frisch said, adding that delinquency rates were fairly steady.

Wall Street executives and experts have said that while spending trends have largely remained stable, higher gasoline prices spurred by the war could start impacting some categories.

The conflict has also shrouded travel demand, as the Middle East turmoil forced airspace closures and disrupted key global flight corridors.

data.title: TRIMAS CORP 1Q 2026: Revenue $168.28M, Net income $800.83M, EPS $21.4— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
TRIMAS CORP reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue of $168.28M, up 10.4% from $152.46M a year earlier, and diluted EPS of $21.4 versus $0.3 in the prior-year quarter as net income rose to $800.83M from $12.42M.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$168.28M$152.46M10.4%
Net income²$800.83M$12.42M6347.9%
Diluted EPS³$21.4$0.37033.3%

¹ Reported as “Net sales”. ² Reported as “Net income”. ³ Reported as “income per share”.

Business Highlights

  • Net sales rose 10.4% year over year driven by 7.3% organic growth across segments.
  • Packaging segment saw steady demand with 4.4% organic growth supported by operational improvements and life‑sciences & dispensing product growth.
  • Specialty Products delivered strong organic growth of 22.6%, led by steel cylinders.
  • Company completed an Aerospace divestiture and is executing a Packaging and corporate realignment expected to generate more than $10M in savings in 2026; the realignment included one‑time severance and restructuring costs.
  • Working capital increased as inventory and receivables were built to support demand.

Original SEC Filing: TRIMAS CORP [ TRS ] - 10-Q - Apr. 30, 2026

data.title: BELDEN INC. 1Q 2026: Revenue $696.4M, EPS $1.3— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
BELDEN INC. reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue rising to $696.4M and diluted EPS of $1.3, while net income was $51M, roughly in line with the year-ago quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue was $696.4M for Q1 2026, compared with $624.9M in the prior-year quarter; YoY change 11.4%.
  • Net income was $51.0M for Q1 2026, compared with $51.9M in the prior-year quarter; YoY change (1.8%).
  • Diluted EPS was $1.30 for Q1 2026, compared with $1.27 in the prior-year quarter; YoY change 2.4%.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth was driven by higher volume, copper pass-through pricing and favorable currency translation.
  • Higher receivables and an increase in days sales outstanding (60 to 63) pressured operating cash flow and reflected channel inventory dynamics.
  • The company realigned to a unified functional model to accelerate a solutions-first strategy and capture IT/OT convergence opportunities.
  • SG&A and R&D spending increased to support strategic investments, product development and the transition toward solutions and market-share gains.
  • BELDEN entered a definitive agreement to acquire Ruckus to expand its wireless solutions for enterprise and service-provider customers.

Original SEC Filing: BELDEN INC. [ BDC ] - 10-Q - Apr. 30, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Clearfield Extends Credit Line Maturity to July 24, 2026 With Old National Bank
data.description:
Clearfield entered into Amendment No. 3 to its Loan Agreement with Old National Bank on April 25, 2026, extending the maturity of its revolving line of credit to July 24, 2026. The amendment also incorporates updated interest and payment provisions previously reflected in the promissory note, adds certain new events of default, and includes a jury trial waiver. All other material terms remain unchanged. The company expects the amendment to preserve liquidity and financial flexibility while aligning terms with current needs.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Amendment to revolving line of credit under Loan Agreement
  • Counterparty: Old National Bank
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 25 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Through Jul 24 2026
  • Reason: Maintain credit availability and update terms

Original SEC Filing: Clearfield, Inc. [ CLFD ] - 8-K - Apr. 30, 2026
